name: La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge
name_original: Cascada de La Palia — ida y vuelta desde el puente de Valdoradas
region_intro: Hiking around Carbajales De Alba offers diverse terrain characterized by significant hydrological features and varied natural landscapes. The region features the immense Ricobayo Dam and its reservoir on the Esla River, providing panoramic views within a deep geographical depression. Hikers can explore river valleys like the Aliste and the Montes de Cantadores mountain range, with the area also bordering the Parque Natural de los Arribes del Duero. These elements combine to create a landscape suitable for various hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Carbajales De Alba The most popular hiking route is Route of the Perones and Cadozos waterfall — circular from Ricobayo de Alba, a 4.8 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 10 minutes to complete. This route features a seasonal waterfall and views of the Ricobayo Reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillo de Alba de Aliste Trail, a moderate 4.9 miles (8.0 km) path. This trail leads to the ruins of Alba de Aliste castle, offering historical interest along the way. Local hikers also love the Alba Castle – Castle of Alba de Aliste loop from Los Milanos, a 4.3 miles (6.9 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 57 minutes. Hiking around Carbajales De Alba is defined by river gorges, expansive reservoirs, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Carbajales De Alba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 6 reviews. More than 26 hikers have used komoot to explore Carbajales De Alba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Carbajales De Alba?
A: There are 6 hiking routes documented around Carbajales De Alba, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls to more challenging treks. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars.

Q: Are there any hikes with waterfalls in the area?
A: Yes, the region features beautiful seasonal waterfalls. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026677750/route-of-the-perones-and-cadozos-waterfall-circular-from-ricobayo-de-alba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Perones and Cadozos waterfall — circular from Ricobayo de Alba&lt;/a&gt;, which includes views of the Cadozos Waterfall. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026734793/la-palia-waterfall-round-trip-from-valdoradas-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge&lt;/a&gt;, an easy route that also leads to a waterfall.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see on the trails?
A: The hiking trails around Carbajales De Alba are characterized by significant hydrological features and varied natural landscapes. You'll encounter the immense Ricobayo Dam and its reservoir on the Esla River, offering panoramic views within a deep geographical depression. River valleys like the Aliste and the Montes de Cantadores mountain range are also prominent. The area is also close to the Parque Natural de los Arribes del Duero, known for its stunning canyons.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, several routes incorporate historical elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282989725/castillo-de-alba-de-aliste-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Alba de Aliste Trail&lt;/a&gt; leads to the ruins of Alba de Aliste castle, offering a glimpse into the region's past. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844702" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alba Castle – Castle of Alba de Aliste loop from Los Milanos&lt;/a&gt;, which also explores the castle area.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Carbajales De Alba?
A: Spring and autumn are generally recommended seasons for hiking around Carbajales De Alba. During these times, temperatures are pleasant, allowing you to avoid the peak heat of summer and enjoy the natural beauty comfortably.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026677750/route-of-the-perones-and-cadozos-waterfall-circular-from-ricobayo-de-alba" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Perones and Cadozos waterfall — circular from Ricobayo de Alba&lt;/a&gt;. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844702" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alba Castle – Castle of Alba de Aliste loop from Los Milanos&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a loop through rural landscapes.

Q: What are some notable attractions to explore near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can visit several significant attractions. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Dam&lt;/a&gt; is a major hydroelectric project with spectacular views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442442" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ricobayo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; itself is vast and offers panoramic vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdoradas Bridge&lt;/a&gt; provides excellent views of the reservoir and is a convenient starting point for some routes. You can also find the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5442448" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cadozos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, the area offers easy routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1026734793/la-palia-waterfall-round-trip-from-valdoradas-bridge" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Palia Waterfall - round trip from Valdoradas bridge&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.4 km hike. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31844702" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alba Castle – Castle of Alba de Aliste loop from Los Milanos&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.9 km and rated as easy.

Q: What is the most challenging hike in the area?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1123665143/puente-de-valdelasmozas-from-el-campillo-circular-via-almendra-and-andavias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Valdelasmozas from El Campillo – circular via Almendra and Andavias&lt;/a&gt; is rated as difficult. This route covers over 25 km and involves significant elevation changes, making it suitable for experienced hikers.

Q: Is there parking available at trailheads?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, the Valdoradas Bridge, which serves as a starting point for some routes, is known to have parking available nearby. It's advisable to check individual route descriptions for more precise parking information.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Carbajales De Alba?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from 6 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the stunning views of the Ricobayo Reservoir and Dam, and the tranquil river valleys. The combination of natural beauty and historical interest, such as the castle ruins, also receives positive feedback.
tips: Nice bridge over the Ricobayo reservoir that offers us excellent views of it and the entire environment that surrounds us. A few km away is the La Palia Waterfall, well known by nature lovers in the area. On one side of the bridge there is a parking lot where we can leave the car and start our route.
We will find beautiful panoramic views walking along the road near the Valdoradas bridge, surely on our route to the La Palia waterfall.

The La Palia waterfall is a charming place located in the municipality of Videmala and near the Ricobayo reservoir. It is recommended to go in the rainy season or spring to see the waterfall in all its splendor, even so, it is worth visiting any time of the year since it offers us a unique atmosphere. Easy access from the ZA-911 road
